pokemon:dragalge.name: Dragalge
pokemon:dragalge.summary: Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.
Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.
Dragalge generates dragon energy by sticking the plume on its head out above the ocean’s surface and bathing it in sunlight.
Dragalge uses a poisonous liquid capable of corroding metal to send tankers that enter its territory to the bottom of the sea.
It blends in with seaweed to ambush its prey and then takes them down with a poisonous liquid strong enough to melt metal.
This vicious Pokémon sprays a poisonous liquid on opponents that come near. For whatever reason, it gets along really well with Dhelmise.
Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.
Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.

pokemon:skrelp.name: Skrelp
pokemon:skrelp.summary: It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.
Camouflaged as rotten kelp, they spray liquid poison on prey that approaches unawares and then finish it off.
Skrelp looks like a piece of rotten seaweed, so it can blend in with seaweed drifting on the ocean and avoid being detected by enemies.
It drifts in the ocean, blending in with floating seaweed. When other Pokémon come to feast on the seaweed, Skrelp feasts on them instead.
It’s not good at swimming, so it clings to the seaweed. In the Alola region, it’s often found near Dhelmise.
It vanishes into seaweed and remains perfectly still to avoid attacks from large Pokémon. Rotten seaweed is its main food source.
Camouflaged as rotten kelp, they spray liquid poison on prey that approaches unawares and then finish it off.
It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.
